title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- New Zealand Health Strategy 2016 Publication date: 18 April 2016 This publication presents the New Zealand government’s refreshed strategy for services to improve the health of New Zealand people and communities. It has two parts: Future Direction lays the 10-year vision for the Strategy and the five themes that guides the Strategy; and Roadmap of Actions 2016 that identifies 27 areas for action to make the Strategy happen. Publication Health system

## New Zealand Health Strategy 2016
This publication presents the New Zealand government’s refreshed strategy for services to improve the health of New Zealand people and communities. It has two parts: Future Direction lays the 10-year vision for the Strategy and the five themes that guides the Strategy; and Roadmap of Actions 2016 that identifies 27 areas for action to make the Strategy happen.
- Section 99 Inspection of Waikato District Health Board Mental Health and Addiction Services Publication date: 15 April 2016 In early 2015, there were a number of serious events at the Waikato District Health Board mental health and addiction services. Following these events, Dr John Crawshaw, Director of Mental Health, inspected the services to ensure they are providing a good quality of care to people in the Waikato region. This document is the final report from that inspection. Publication Mental health

## Section 99 Inspection of Waikato District Health Board Mental Health and Addiction Services
In early 2015, there were a number of serious events at the Waikato District Health Board mental health and addiction services. Following these events, Dr John Crawshaw, Director of Mental Health, inspected the services to ensure they are providing a good quality of care to people in the Waikato region. This document is the final report from that inspection.
- Exploring the Links between Quality Improvement Strategies and Organisational Outcomes in Four New Zealand District Health Boards: Final Report Publication date: 15 April 2016 This publication reports on the results of a project which examined the links between quality improvement strategies implemented by four New Zealand district health boards and their organisational outcomes. Publication Crown entities & agencies

## Exploring the Links between Quality Improvement Strategies and Organisational Outcomes in Four New Zealand District Health Boards: Final Report
This publication reports on the results of a project which examined the links between quality improvement strategies implemented by four New Zealand district health boards and their organisational outcomes.
- Crown entities & agencies
- Review of Physical Activity Guidance and Resources for Under-Fives Publication date: 14 April 2016 The Review of Physical Activity Guidance and Resources for Under-Fives provides options and recommendations to the Ministry on how its Active Movement resources could be updated. Publication Babies and toddlers (0–2 years) Child health Physical activity

## Review of Physical Activity Guidance and Resources for Under-Fives
The Review of Physical Activity Guidance and Resources for Under-Fives provides options and recommendations to the Ministry on how its Active Movement resources could be updated.
- Babies and toddlers (0–2 years)
- Research Report: The New Zealand Drug Harm Index 2016 Publication date: 7 April 2016 The New Zealand Drug Harm Index (DHI) measures the impact of interventions designed to reduce the harms associated with illicit drug use. It also provides an estimate of the total harm associated with the use of illicit drugs. Publication Drugs Mental health Statistics

## Research Report: The New Zealand Drug Harm Index 2016
The New Zealand Drug Harm Index (DHI) measures the impact of interventions designed to reduce the harms associated with illicit drug use. It also provides an estimate of the total harm associated with the use of illicit drugs.
- Our Older People’s Oral Health: Key Findings of the 2012 New Zealand Older People’s Oral Health Survey Publication date: 1 April 2016 This publication reports on the oral health status and experiences of older people living in rest homes or receiving home-based services in the community. It presents comprehensive information on the oral health status of the most vulnerable older New Zealanders. Publication Oral health

## Our Older People’s Oral Health: Key Findings of the 2012 New Zealand Older People’s Oral Health Survey
This publication reports on the oral health status and experiences of older people living in rest homes or receiving home-based services in the community. It presents comprehensive information on the oral health status of the most vulnerable older New Zealanders.
- Population-based Funding Formula Review: 2015 Technical Report Publication date: 24 March 2016 This report describes the 2014/15 Review of the Population-based Funding Formula (PBFF). Publication Funding & payments Crown entities & agencies

## Population-based Funding Formula Review: 2015 Technical Report
This report describes the 2014/15 Review of the Population-based Funding Formula (PBFF).
- Crown entities & agencies
- Evaluation of the Pharmacy Accuracy Checking Technician Demonstration Publication date: 22 March 2016 The aim of the demonstration was to test the concept of enabling a pharmacist more time to provide improved patient services without compromising public safety in the dispensing of medicines Publication Workforce

## Evaluation of the Pharmacy Accuracy Checking Technician Demonstration
The aim of the demonstration was to test the concept of enabling a pharmacist more time to provide improved patient services without compromising public safety in the dispensing of medicines
- Formative Evaluation of Sore Throat Clinics Publication date: 9 March 2016 In July 2015 the Ministry contracted an independent evaluation of the sore throat clinic services component of the Rheumatic Fever Prevention Programme during August/September 2015. This publication contains the findings of this evaluation. Publication Long-term conditions

## Formative Evaluation of Sore Throat Clinics
In July 2015 the Ministry contracted an independent evaluation of the sore throat clinic services component of the Rheumatic Fever Prevention Programme during August/September 2015. This publication contains the findings of this evaluation.
- Long-term conditions
- Smartphone-based Problem Gambling Evaluation and Technology Testing Initiative (‘SPGETTI’) Feasibility Study Publication date: 1 March 2016 This study developed and tested a novel smartphone application to see if it was a feasible way to provide additional support to people with gambling problems associated with pokies. Publication Gambling research Gambling harm

## Smartphone-based Problem Gambling Evaluation and Technology Testing Initiative (‘SPGETTI’) Feasibility Study
This study developed and tested a novel smartphone application to see if it was a feasible way to provide additional support to people with gambling problems associated with pokies.
- Evaluation of the Establishment of Clinical Exercise Physiologist role in New Zealand Publication date: 17 February 2016 This publication is an evaluation report of the establishment of the Clinical Exercise Physiologist role in New Zealand Publication Workforce

## Evaluation of the Establishment of Clinical Exercise Physiologist role in New Zealand
This publication is an evaluation report of the establishment of the Clinical Exercise Physiologist role in New Zealand
- A Best Practice Guide for Continuous Practice Improvement Publication date: 16 February 2016 This guide presents a framework of agreed principles and processes for stakeholders and medical specialists to use when developing programmes and reviewing systems that enable medical specialists in New Zealand to demonstrate their competence by participating in a range of activities that are likely to improve their performance. Publication Hospitals & specialist care Workforce

## A Best Practice Guide for Continuous Practice Improvement
This guide presents a framework of agreed principles and processes for stakeholders and medical specialists to use when developing programmes and reviewing systems that enable medical specialists in New Zealand to demonstrate their competence by participating in a range of activities that are likely to improve their performance.
- Hospitals & specialist care
- Health of the Health Workforce Report 2015 Publication date: 15 February 2016 This report is Health Workforce New Zealand's second update on the state of the health and disability workforce Publication Workforce

## Health of the Health Workforce Report 2015
This report is Health Workforce New Zealand's second update on the state of the health and disability workforce
- Pertussis Control Strategies 2015: A consistent approach for New Zealand Publication date: 9 February 2016 This publication summarises the discussions by pertussis subject matter experts at the April 2015 Ministry of Health Workshop on pertussis disease control strategies to develop a consistent approach for New Zealand. Publication Immunisation

## Pertussis Control Strategies 2015: A consistent approach for New Zealand
This publication summarises the discussions by pertussis subject matter experts at the April 2015 Ministry of Health Workshop on pertussis disease control strategies to develop a consistent approach for New Zealand.
- Indicators for the Well Child/ Tamariki Ora Quality Improvement Framework – September 2015 Publication date: 4 February 2016 This report, the fifth in the series, identifies variable clinical practice, service quality and health outcomes across Well Child / Tamariki Ora and related child health services. Publication Babies and toddlers (0–2 years) Child health Health system

## Indicators for the Well Child/ Tamariki Ora Quality Improvement Framework – September 2015
This report, the fifth in the series, identifies variable clinical practice, service quality and health outcomes across Well Child / Tamariki Ora and related child health services.
